channel.title: BNN Bloomberg
channel.description: BNN Bloomberg is Canada's only television service devoted exclusively to business and finance news with wall-to-wall coverage of the markets.

item.description:
Pedro Antunes, chief economist at Conference Board of Canada, joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss negative domestic growth in December. He says wages help offset some of the erosion that inflation has had on housing purchasing power.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg
Connect with BNN Bloomberg: For the latest news visit: https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca For a full video offering visit BNN Bloomberg: https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca/video BNN Bloomberg on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/BNNBloomberg BNN Bloomberg on Twitter: https://twitter.com/bnnbloomberg BNN Bloomberg on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/bnnbloomberg BNN Bloomberg on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/bnn-bloomberg -- BNN Bloomberg is Canada’s only TV service devoted exclusively to business, finance and the markets.
item.title: BREAKING: Canada's economy grows less than expected in December
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
BNN Bloomberg's Amber Kanwar looks at a chart and talks about the two common indicators of inflation: CPI and PCE.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.title: Two indicators of inflation | Chart Attack
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Vitali Mossounov, Global Technology Analyst at TDAM returns to MoneyTalk to discuss big tech layoffs
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.title: MoneyTalk - Big Tech Layoffs
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Phil Soper, president and CEO of Royal LePage, joins BNN Bloomberg to talk about housing trends in Canada. He says decades of underbuilding relative to population growth has created a big problem, and the post-pandemic market correction could be short-lived as Canada navigates record levels of immigration.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg
Connect with BNN Bloomberg: For the latest news visit: https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca For a full video offering visit BNN Bloomberg: https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca/video BNN Bloomberg on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/BNNBloomberg BNN Bloomberg on Twitter: https://twitter.com/bnnbloomberg BNN Bloomberg on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/bnnbloomberg BNN Bloomberg on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/bnn-bloomberg -- BNN Bloomberg is Canada’s only TV service devoted exclusively to business, finance and the markets.
item.title: The post-pandemic housing correction is a brief reprieve
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Bryden Teich, partner and portfolio manager at Avenue Investment Management, joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss his outlook for markets and where he sees investing opportunities. He looks at high quality businesses that are in a good position to maintain their profitability while at the same time using their cash flow to repurchase shares.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.title: The forward looking indicators are suggesting a recession lays soon ahead
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
James Orlando, senior economist at TD, joins MoneyTalk to discuss the Canadian housing market.

item.title: MoneyTalk - Outlook for Canada’s Housing Market
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Pedro Palandrani, VP and director of research at Global X ETFs, joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss his top three stock picks in AI. Palandrani is the director of research for Global X's AI ETFs: AIQ and BOTZ, and talks about why he sees value in this sector. He recommends Nvidia, Google and Baidu as his top names to ride the AI wave.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.title: 3 stocks to play AI growth: Hot Picks
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Laura Lau, chief investment officer of Brompton Funds, joins BNN Bloomberg to share her hot picks in food stocks. Lau says that inflation can be a beneficial tool for food manufacturers and grocery stores, as they can often pass costs onto consumers.

item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Chris Weafer, general director and CEO of Macro-Advisory LLC, joins BNN Bloomberg to talk about Russia's economy since it invaded Ukraine a year ago. He says predictions of a rapid economic decline and a budget crisis have proven to be greatly exaggerated.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.title: The delay of oil sanctions allowed Russia's economy to keep ticking over: Chris Weafer
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Bruce Sellery, CEO of Credit Canada joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss how one can prepare for and overcome sudden job loss. He says to 'fill the well before you're thirsty' and that there are ways to prepare for a layoff financially, practically and emotionally.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Mark Sebastian, founder of OptionPit.com and CIO of Karman Line Capital and financial expert Frances Horodelski, join BNN Bloomberg to discuss how the markets will perform this year. Sebastian says that he expects to see a declining volatility market and gives his top stock picks for bullish swells.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
As rates stay high, what's the best strategy on mortgage renewals? Guest: Janet Gray, financial plann and James Laird, co-CEO, Ratehub.ca
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.title: Taking Stock - Millennial's are up to their necks in debt
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Victoria Fernandez, chief market strategist at Crossmark Global Investments, joins BNN Bloomberg for her view on the stock market. Fernandez says the elements that support the economy do not help central banks’ inflation fight. Fernandez also discusses Nordstrom winding down Canadian operations, advising investors to be selective when choosing retail names as some will do better than others. Furthermore, she likes staples and cyclical names and advises not to make big bets in this market, instead focusing on a balanced portfolio.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg
Connect with BNN Bloomberg: For the latest news visit: https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca For a full video offering visit BNN Bloomberg: https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca/video BNN Bloomberg on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/BNNBloomberg BNN Bloomberg on Twitter: https://twitter.com/bnnbloomberg BNN Bloomberg on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/bnnbloomberg BNN Bloomberg on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/bnn-bloomberg -- BNN Bloomberg is Canada’s only TV service devoted exclusively to business, finance and the markets.
item.title: No need to make big bets in this market just yet
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Jim Thorne, chief market strategist at Wellington-Altus Private Wealth, joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss his views on if rates will stay higher for longer. Thorne remains firm that the fed will pause, then it will cut, we are near disinflation. He discusses his thesis and says disinflation will be clear by the spring and that he's buying the dips in these markets to benefit.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.title: Canada does not have an inflation problem: Jim Thorne
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
David Nelson, chief strategist at Belpointe Asset Management, joins BNN Bloomberg for his view on the stock market amid China's reopening. Nelson likes names the EV industry and is bullish on Albemarle Corporation. He also likes Health Care and advises investors to look at XLV.

item.description:
Jennifer Fiddian-Green, forensics partner at Grant Thornton, joins BNN Bloomberg to share examples of how Canadian seniors are being targeted for financial scams. She says fraudsters use advanced technologies and research social media profiles to lure vulnerable seniors.

item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Dominique Lapointe, global macro strategist at Manulife Investment Management, joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss Canada's inflation rate that slow to 5.9 per cent in January. He says retail consumption on nominal basis is still miles away from pre-pandemic trends.

item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Roger McKnight, chief petroleum analyst at En-Pro International, joins BNN Bloomberg to give his gasoline price outlook for the week ahead. McKnight noted that 'over the short term pump prices are under downward pressure as crude oil and gasoline inventories continue to build.' McKnight added that there's no specific timeline on when this will change.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.title: Gasoline price outlook: Oil price drop
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Iman Brivanlou, managing director of income equities at TCW, joins BNN Bloomberg for his three top picks in dividend stocks. Brivanlou says that dividend stocks are attractive right now as they can provide protection until the markets rebound. He likes Ares Capital, Alexandria Real Estate Equities and M&T Bank.

item.description:
Phil Davis returns to MoneyTalk to discuss some picks that could benefit from widespread AI use.

item.title: MoneyTalk - Investing in ChatGPT's AI Revolution
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Chris Kerlow, senior portfolio manager and investment advisor, Team LWC, at Canaccord Genuity, joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss his reaction to RBC's earnings this morning. Kerlow discusses all of the big 6 banks, saying he's rather impressed by RBC and TD's strategies this quarter but favours Scotiabank for his portfolio right now. He dives into the stock, management and M&A news of the respective companies.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.title: Expect a long-term bullish run for Canadian banks if there isn't a deep recession
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Can Canadians cope with higher interest rates? Benjamin Tal, deputy chief economist, CIBC.

item.description:
Guest: Perry Wright, Centre for Advanced Hindsight, Duke University. Takeaway: We have looked the other way on high-interest loans.

item.description:
Brianne Gardner, wealth manager and investment advisor at Velocity Investment Partners with Raymond James, joins BNN Bloomberg to talk about her outlook for markets. She says the fear of missing out is driving the market right now, but investors should be careful.

item.description:
Roy Gori, president and CEO of Manulife, joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss China reopening, the rise on travel insurance demand amid post-pandemic travel boom. He also discusses his view on climate change. Ottawa's tax measures and economic uncertainty.

item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Jeff Weniger, head of equity strategy at WisdomTree Asset Management, joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss why he thinks market volatility is heading higher. He also says the freeze-up in U.S. housing is a key driver for labor market deterioration.

item.description:
Nordstrom, Inc. announced it is winding down Canadian operations, saying they “do not see a realistic path to profitability” for the business.

item.description:
Stephen Lezak, commodities and infrastructure sustainability programme manager at the University of Oxford, joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss his research on how gold mining causes global social and ecological harm, while contributing little to human welfare.

item.title: Making the case to recycle gold instead of mining it
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Brian Belski, chief market strategist at BMO, joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss the market. Belski tells BNN Bloomberg January ended off on a strong note for the U.S stocks, but that doesn’t mean things remained the same throughout this month. He added that the TSX is well positioned for continued outperformance, versus the U.S and other global markets.
Subscribe to BNN Bloomberg to watch more videos: https://www.youtube.com/BNNBloomberg

item.title: TSX is well positioned for continued outperformance, versus the U.S.
item.author: BNN Bloomberg

item.description:
Josh Young, CIO of Bison Interests, joins BNN Bloomberg to provide a recap of energy earnings including Canadian Natural Resources, Crescent Point and Tourmaline Oil. He says there's opportunity for small cap oil & gas equities as they trade at a material discount to larger cap peers.
